name: Flapping Airplanes
founders: Ben Spector, Asher Spector, Aidan Smith (NOTE: Alexi Gladstone and Sally Zhu are NOT confirmed founders; no public connection found between them and this company)
what_it_does: Foundational AI research lab building data-efficient AI systems that learn more like humans. Aims to replace current training paradigms with fundamentally new approaches — new loss functions, possibly replacing gradient descent.
problem_solved: Current AI models are 100,000x to 1,000,000x less data-efficient than humans. Flapping Airplanes is researching radically different architectures to close that gap.
funding: $180M seed round from Google Ventures, Sequoia, and Index Ventures
traction: Major TechCrunch coverage; backing from three top-tier firms at seed. Early-stage research lab — no product yet.
differentiator: Pursuing fundamental research into data efficiency rather than scaling existing paradigms. Named after the idea that copying biology (flapping wings) isn't the right path — you need first-principles engineering.
delta_fellow_connection: UNVERIFIED — Alexi Gladstone (UIUC PhD) and Sally Zhu were listed as Delta Fellows but no public evidence connects them to Flapping Airplanes. Alexi is a PhD student at UIUC focused on world models and energy-based transformers.

name: Periodic Labs
founders: Liam Fedus (ex-VP Post-training at OpenAI), Ekin Dogus Cubuk (ex-Head of Quantum Materials at Google DeepMind). Rohan Pandey is an early team member, not a founder.
what_it_does: Automating scientific discovery by creating AI scientists paired with robotic labs that conduct physical experiments, collect data, iterate, and repeat.
problem_solved: Scientific discovery is slow and human-bottlenecked. Periodic Labs aims to use AI + robotics to dramatically accelerate materials science research, starting with superconductors.
funding: $300M seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz, with Felicis cutting the first check
traction: Came out of stealth Sept 2025. One of the largest seed rounds in AI history. Initial focus on inventing new superconductors.
differentiator: Combines frontier AI (from team that built ChatGPT and GNoME) with physical robotics labs — not just computational science but actual wet-lab automation.
delta_fellow_connection: Rohan Pandey (prev OpenAI, CMU grad) is a team member working on autonomous science, not a founder.

name: Decagon
founders: Jesse Zhang, Ashwin Sreenivas (co-founders). Cynthia Chen and Amy Jin are engineers, not co-founders.
what_it_does: Leading conversational AI platform for customer experience — AI agents that handle chat, email, and voice support for enterprises across every language.
problem_solved: Customer support is expensive, inconsistent, and doesn't scale. Decagon's AI agents resolve millions of inquiries with human-like quality at any time.
funding: $250M round at $4.5B valuation, led by Coatue and Index Ventures. Previous backers include a16z, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures.
traction: Valued at $4.5B. Serves major enterprises. Opened NYC office. Millions of customer interactions resolved.
differentiator: Enterprise-grade AI agents for CX that are deeply integrated and customizable per brand, not generic chatbots.
delta_fellow_connection: Cynthia Chen (Harvard CS, engineer) and Amy Jin (engineer/ML) are team members at Decagon, not founders.

name: Thinking Machines Lab
founders: Mira Murati (ex-OpenAI CTO), John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng, Andrew Tulloch, Luke Metz. Kevin Lu joined as a researcher, not a co-founder.
what_it_does: Frontier AI lab building customizable, generally capable AI systems. First product 'Tinker' lets developers fine-tune models without expensive distributed training.
problem_solved: Making AI more widely understood, customizable, and generally capable — moving beyond one-size-fits-all foundation models.
funding: $2B seed at $12B valuation led by a16z, with Nvidia, Accel, ServiceNow, AMD, Jane Street. In talks for $5B+ at ~$50B valuation.
traction: One of largest seed rounds in SV history. Launched Tinker (Oct 2025). Plans to release own models in 2026. Some co-founder turbulence (Zoph left for OpenAI).
differentiator: Dream team from OpenAI + strong public benefit corporation structure. Murati has unusual voting control. Focus on model customization/fine-tuning as first product.
delta_fellow_connection: Kevin Lu (ex-OpenAI, led 4o-mini) joined as researcher. Expert in RL, synthetic data, small models. UC Berkeley background.

name: Rox
founders: Ishan Mukherjee (CEO), Avanika Narayan (AI Lead), Diogo Ribeiro, Shriram Sridharan, Chris Re
what_it_does: AI-native Salesforce — uses AI agent swarms to augment sellers with planning, research, and engagement automation.
problem_solved: Sales reps spend too much time on manual research, planning, and admin rather than actually selling. Rox reclaims 8+ hours/week per AE.
funding: $50M from Sequoia, GV, and General Catalyst
traction: 35+ enterprise sales teams including MongoDB, OpenAI, Ramp. AEs see 35%+ increase in customer activity.
differentiator: Founded by ex-Siri product lead + Stanford CS PhD (Avanika) + Chris Re. Deep AI/data expertise applied to sales workflows specifically.
delta_fellow_connection: Avanika Narayan (Stanford CS PhD, Knight-Hennessy Scholar) is co-founder and AI Lead. NOTE: Austin Chen, Jerry Zhou, Nikita Mounier were not found in public sources as Rox team members.

name: Design Arena
founders: Grace Li (CEO), Kamryn Ohly (CTO), Jayden Personnat (Chief AI/ML). Parent company: Arcada Labs.
what_it_does: World's largest crowdsourced benchmark for AI-generated design. Presents side-by-side AI outputs and uses Elo ratings from human votes to rank models on taste, usability, and aesthetics.
problem_solved: No standard exists to measure AI design quality (taste, usability, aesthetics). Without benchmarks there's no pressure for models to improve at design tasks.
funding: Y Combinator S25 batch. No additional funding publicly disclosed.
traction: 47,000+ users in first month. 35K+ users across 135 countries in first 3 weeks. Evaluates frontend, audio, image, video and more.
differentiator: Crowdsourced Elo-based benchmarking for subjective AI quality (design/aesthetics) — similar to Chatbot Arena but for visual/creative outputs.
delta_fellow_connection: Grace Li (Harvard CS/Neuroscience, ex-Apple) is CEO and co-founder.

name: a37 (Applied37)
founders: Rohan Kalahasty (CEO), Sritan Motati, Tahsin Zaman, Brian Zhou
what_it_does: AI-native DevOps workspace ('Forge') with AI agents that reason across entire enterprise DevOps stacks — provisioning cloud resources, optimizing CI/CD, managing infrastructure.
problem_solved: AI has accelerated code production but infrastructure management remains manual. a37 bridges the gap between fast development and slow, manual DevOps.
funding: Unclear — Tracxn says unfunded, PitchBook lists as VC-backed. Likely early seed stage.
traction: Open-sourced Cloudscript (next-gen IaC language). Second-time founders from TJHSST. Early stage.
differentiator: AI agents that understand complex dependencies across entire DevOps stacks, not just individual tools. Founded by repeat technical entrepreneurs.
delta_fellow_connection: Rohan Kalahasty (Caltech Physics, prev founded Vytal valued at $12.5M at age ~17) is CEO.

name: Applied Compute
founders: Rhythm Garg, Linden Li, Yash Patil (all ex-OpenAI, Stanford grads). NOTE: Oam Patel was not found as a founder.
what_it_does: Builds custom AI agents trained on enterprise-specific data through embedded engineering teams. Creates RL environments for companies to train proprietary AI agents.
problem_solved: Enterprises need AI trained on their own data and workflows, not generic models. Applied Compute provides the infrastructure and team to make that happen.
funding: Total ~$100M raised. $20M seed led by Benchmark (June 2025), $80M Series B from Sequoia/Lux/Benchmark (Oct 2025). In talks at $1.3B valuation (Jan 2026).
traction: Clients include DoorDash, Mercor, Cognition (Devin). 18 employees, 2/3 are former founders. Rapid valuation growth: $100M -> $500M -> $1.3B in months.
differentiator: Embedded engineering model — they work alongside enterprise teams. Core team built OpenAI's o1 reasoning model and Codex agent.
delta_fellow_connection: UNVERIFIED — Oam Patel (Harvard Stats+CS) may be a team member but was not found as a public founder. The confirmed founders are Garg, Li, and Patil.

name: Cogent Security
founders: Vineet Edupuganti (CEO), Geng Sng (CTO), Thanos Baskous (VP Eng). Nina Boord is a Forward Deployed Engineer, not a founder.
what_it_does: AI-powered vulnerability management platform using agentic AI to autonomously assess infrastructure risk, prioritize threats, coordinate fixes, and generate reports.
problem_solved: Security teams are overwhelmed by vulnerabilities and manual triage. Cogent automates the entire vulnerability management lifecycle with AI agents.
funding: $11M seed led by Greylock (incubated via Edge programme). $42M Series A led by Bain Capital Ventures. Total ~$53M.
traction: Already used by Fortune 500 companies, public firms, and major universities. Significantly reduces manual workload for security analysts.
differentiator: First company to deliver an AI taskforce for vulnerability management. Founded by team that scaled Abnormal AI from early days + Coinbase/Blackstone infrastructure expertise.
delta_fellow_connection: Nina Boord (Stanford CS/AI, prev Nuro) is a Forward Deployed Engineer at Cogent, not a founder.

name: NeoSigma
founders: Gauri Gupta (Founder), Ritvik Kapila (co-founder, UCSD)
what_it_does: Custom evaluation and post-training for real-world AI agents. Builds tools for evaluating and improving AI agents deployed in production environments.
problem_solved: AI agents operating in real-world environments need specialized evaluation and post-training — generic benchmarks don't capture real-world agent performance.
funding: Not publicly disclosed. Early stage. Gauri went through O-1 visa process suggesting US-based fundraising.
traction: Early stage. Hiring researchers with post-training experience. Gauri has presented research publicly and is building the team.
differentiator: Deep research expertise (MIT Media Lab, IIT Delhi) applied specifically to the agent evaluation and post-training niche.
delta_fellow_connection: Gauri Gupta (IIT Delhi, MIT Media Lab) is the founder.

name: Haize Labs
founders: Leonard Tang (CEO), Steve Li, Richard Liu — all Harvard undergrads
what_it_does: AI safety startup that automates red-teaming of AI models. Develops algorithmic suites to discover vulnerabilities and jailbreaks in LLMs before deployment.
problem_solved: AI models ship with exploitable safety vulnerabilities. Manual red-teaming doesn't scale. Haize automates adversarial testing to find and close loopholes.
funding: Series A led by General Catalyst at ~$100M valuation (Aug 2024). Additional $12.5M seed round (Dec 2025). Total ~$20M raised.
traction: Clients include Anthropic. Red-teamed OpenAI's o1/o1-mini. Forbes 30 Under 30 AI (2025). 14 employees. Acts as 'Moody's for AI' providing safety ratings.
differentiator: Algorithmic approach to jailbreaking — their Haize Suite autonomously discovers novel attack vectors without human intervention. Multi-turn attacks are a specialty.
delta_fellow_connection: Leonard Tang (Harvard CS/Math, dropped out of Stanford PhD) is CEO and co-founder.

name: Voyage AI
founders: Tengyu Ma (CEO/founder). Patrick Wang appears to be an employee, not a founder.
what_it_does: Builds state-of-the-art embedding models and rerankers for search and retrieval, with domain-specific models for code, legal, and financial services.
problem_solved: RAG systems need high-quality embeddings to reduce hallucinations. Generic embeddings miss domain-specific nuance. Voyage provides specialized contextual embeddings.
funding: $28M total ($20M Series A led by CRV with Snowflake, Databricks participation). Acquired by MongoDB (terms undisclosed).
traction: Acquired by MongoDB in Feb 2025. Models integrated into MongoDB Atlas. ~12 employees pre-acquisition.
differentiator: Domain-specific contextual embeddings that capture not just meaning but context. Now part of MongoDB, giving massive distribution advantage.
delta_fellow_connection: Patrick Wang is associated with Voyage AI/MongoDB but appears to be an employee, not a founder.

name: Judgment Labs
founders: Alex Shan (CEO), Joseph Sripramong Camyre, Andrew Li
what_it_does: Agent Behavior Monitoring (ABM) platform — infrastructure to track, evaluate, and improve AI agent reliability in real-time production deployments.
problem_solved: Deploying autonomous AI agents in mission-critical applications (legal, finance, enterprise) is risky without proper monitoring. Judgment provides the observability layer.
funding: Unfunded as of latest data. Early stage.
traction: Open-source framework Judgeval released. One case study: eliminated manual review, enabled 3x faster agent updates, saved 100+ hours of lawyer time/month.
differentiator: Specialized in runtime agent monitoring (not just static eval) — evaluations, tracing, and optimization flywheels for multi-step agent workflows.
delta_fellow_connection: Alex Shan (Stanford MS CS, prev SAIL, Juniper Networks) is co-founder and CEO.

name: Cartesia
founders: Karan Goel, Arjun Desai, Brandon Yang, Albert Gu, Chris Re. Rohan Tibrewal is a Member of Technical Staff, not a founder.
what_it_does: Next-gen AI models based on State Space Models (SSMs) — a fundamental new primitive for training foundation models. Flagship product Sonic is a real-time text-to-speech API.
problem_solved: Transformer-based models are compute-hungry and high-latency. SSMs offer a more efficient architecture for real-time AI across text, audio, video, images, and time-series.
funding: $64M Series A led by Kleiner Perkins, with Index, Lightspeed, Samsung Ventures. Total $91M raised.
traction: 10,000+ customers using Sonic. Cut latency from 90ms to 45ms. Used in customer service, healthcare, gaming, logistics.
differentiator: Invented SSMs as PhD research at Stanford AI Lab. 4+ years of theory + scaling = state-of-the-art across multiple modalities. Albert Gu and Chris Re are world-class researchers.
delta_fellow_connection: Rohan Tibrewal (UC Berkeley CS, prev Jane Street, IMC Trading) is a Member of Technical Staff.

name: Moonlake AI
founders: Fan-Yun Sun, Sharon Lee
what_it_does: AI lab that generates interactive simulations and games from natural language prompts. Combines spatial reasoning, program synthesis, and real-time diffusion rendering.
problem_solved: Current AI generates static images/videos but can't create dynamic, physics-based interactive environments. AI needs simulations to learn through RL — Moonlake builds those worlds.
funding: $28M seed from AIX Ventures, Threshold, and Nvidia Ventures. Angel investors include YouTube co-founder Steve Chen, Naval Ravikant, Ian Goodfellow.
traction: In private alpha, beta planned. Featured in Fast Company. Backed by who's who of AI researchers.
differentiator: Multi-layered approach: multimodal reasoning for layout + program synthesis for interactive logic + real-time diffusion for rendering. Aimed at being foundation for RL training environments.
delta_fellow_connection: Fan-Yun Sun (Stanford AI Lab PhD, ex-Nvidia research) is co-founder.

name: Datacurve
founders: Serena Ge, Charley Lee. NOTE: John Rho was not found as a co-founder in public sources.
what_it_does: Data platform providing expert-quality code data at scale via a 'bounty hunter' system that incentivizes skilled software engineers to generate high-quality training datasets.
problem_solved: The biggest bottleneck in improving vertical LLM capabilities is lack of curated, high-quality training data. Datacurve solves this with incentivized expert data collection.
funding: $15M Series A led by Chemistry (Mark Goldberg), plus $2.7M seed. Total $17.7M. YC W24 graduate.
traction: Hit $1M+ MRR within 2 months of launch. Distributed $1M+ in bounties. Working with DeepMind, Vercel, Anthropic, OpenAI employees as investors.
differentiator: Unique bounty system incentivizes real engineers (not crowd workers) to produce training data. Founded by ex-Cohere/Google interns who trained LLMs firsthand. Waterloo dropouts.
delta_fellow_connection: Serena Ge (Waterloo CS dropout, ex-Cohere intern) is co-founder. John Rho was not found publicly associated.

name: Kinetic Systems
founders: Likely Michael Wornow (stealth). Company name not publicly confirmed.
what_it_does: Likely AI for healthcare workflows based on Wornow's research background — automating clinical workflows, EHR-based tasks, and hospital operations using foundation models.
problem_solved: Healthcare workflows are manual, time-consuming, and error-prone. Wornow's research shows AI can automate enterprise workflows with 93% accuracy and near-instant setup.
funding: Unknown — company is in stealth mode.
traction: Wornow published ECLAIR (end-to-end digital workflow automation) and clinical trial screening with LLMs. NSF and Stanford HAI fellowships. Stealth startup listed on LinkedIn.
differentiator: Deep healthcare AI expertise (Stanford PhD advised by Nigam Shah and Chris Re). Research-backed approach to workflow automation in knowledge-intensive settings.
delta_fellow_connection: Michael Wornow (Harvard summa cum laude, Stanford CS PhD) is likely the founder of this stealth company.

name: AlphaXiv
founders: Rehaan Ahmad, Raj Palleti, Daniel Kim, Lino Le Van
what_it_does: Academic research hub for AI — users replace arxiv.org with alphaxiv.org to get discussion, commenting, AI-driven Q&A, note-taking, and paper recommendations alongside the original paper.
problem_solved: AI research has grown exponentially, making it impossible to stay current. AlphaXiv bridges the gap from research papers to practical application.
funding: $7M seed round
traction: Millions of users across academia and industry since 2024 launch. Uses Mistral OCR and Claude 3.7. Featured in IEEE Spectrum.
differentiator: Dead-simple UX (just change the URL). Crowdsourced academic discussion + AI-powered paper comprehension. Becoming the 'tool layer' for applied AI research.
delta_fellow_connection: Raj Palleti (Stanford, robotics/NLP researcher) is co-founder.

name: Conway
founders: Sudarshan Nambiar and team (10 employees)
what_it_does: AI-powered automation for risk management and security operations — fraud detection, content moderation, AML alert triage, and security threat detection.
problem_solved: Traditional ML models for fraud/security require expensive retraining and generate costly false positives. Conway's vertically integrated AI adapts continuously with near-zero false positive cost.
funding: Funded by 515 Ventures, BoxGroup, First Round Capital, Kleiner Perkins, Roar Ventures. Amount undisclosed.
traction: 10 employees. Backed by top-tier VCs (First Round, Kleiner Perkins). Automating detection, investigation, and decisioning in a single platform.
differentiator: No retraining period, no manual label generation. System adapts as attackers change strategies. Vertically integrated: detection + investigation + decisioning in one platform.
delta_fellow_connection: Sudarshan Nambiar (Purdue CS Engineering senior, ex-Meta intern) is the founder.

name: Avoca
founders: Tyson Chen (co-founder/President), Apurva Shrivastava (co-founder). Rong Ye is in engineering, not a founder.
what_it_does: AI-powered sales agent for home services/trades — answers every call, text, and email using generative AI so businesses never miss a customer.
problem_solved: Small service businesses (HVAC, plumbing, etc.) miss calls and lose customers because they can't staff phones 24/7. Avoca's AI handles all inbound/outbound communication.
funding: YC W23. Estimated ~$47.8M total raised (PitchBook). Investors include Alt Capital, Amino Capital, Audacious Ventures.
traction: 127 employees. Exclusively focused on trades/home services vertical. Both founders have personal connections to small business pain points.
differentiator: Vertical focus on trades/home services rather than general-purpose AI. Deep domain expertise in the specific workflows of service businesses.
delta_fellow_connection: Rong Ye is in an engineering role at Avoca, not a founder.

name: Genesis Molecular AI (Genesis Therapeutics)
founders: Evan Feinberg (CEO/co-founder). Daniel Zeng is a Senior ML Research Engineer, not a founder.
what_it_does: AI-powered drug discovery platform (GEMS) using physics-based ML and foundation models to unlock tough protein targets and invent medicines with unprecedented potency and selectivity.
problem_solved: Drug discovery is slow, expensive, and has high failure rates. Genesis uses AI + molecular simulation to make the process faster and more accurate.
funding: $200M Series B (led by unnamed US life sciences investor + a16z). $52M Series A (2020). Total $250M+.
traction: Founded 2019. Active drug discovery programs. Built on PotentialNet (physics-based ML framework from Stanford). Foundation model 'Pearl' for molecular research.
differentiator: Combines physics-based simulation with AI — not just data-driven but grounded in molecular physics. Strong Stanford pedigree (Vijay Pande lab).
delta_fellow_connection: Daniel Zeng (Stanford MS CS, UC Berkeley BS CS) is a Senior ML Research Engineer focused on generative modeling for drug discovery.

name: Percepta
founders: Hirsh Jain (CEO), Radha Jain (co-founders of the GC-backed Percepta). NOTE: Raj Mehta was not found as a founder.
what_it_does: General Catalyst transformation company helping critical institutions actualize AI transformation. Enterprise AI platform competing with Palantir.
problem_solved: Large institutions struggle to implement AI transformation. Percepta provides hands-on AI transformation services for critical infrastructure.
funding: Backed by General Catalyst as a transformation company. Exact funding not publicly disclosed.
traction: In active lawsuit with Palantir (Palantir alleges trade secret theft). Team from Palantir, Google, MIT, Harvard. Offices in NY, Boston, London.
differentiator: GC transformation model (not traditional VC-backed startup). Direct competition with Palantir in enterprise AI. Founded by alumni with deep Palantir experience.
delta_fellow_connection: UNVERIFIED — Raj Mehta was not found connected to Percepta in public sources.

name: Letta
founders: Sarah Wooders, Charles Packer (UC Berkeley PhD students). Ari Webb is a Member of Technical Staff, not a founder.
what_it_does: Open-source platform for building stateful AI agents with advanced memory that can learn and self-improve over time. Built on the MemGPT research project.
problem_solved: AI agents lack persistent memory — they can't remember users, context, or past interactions. Letta solves this with a memory-first architecture.
funding: $10M seed led by Felicis at $70M post-money valuation.
traction: MemGPT open-source project is widely popular. Launched Letta Code (memory-first coding agent). Released V1.0 SDK in TypeScript and Python. Context-Bench benchmark released.
differentiator: Memory-first approach to agents — fully stateful agents that get better over time. Model-agnostic (works with any LLM). Positioning as 'open alternative to OpenAI.'
delta_fellow_connection: Ari Webb (2025 Qualcomm Innovation Fellow, prev 8vc) is a Member of Technical Staff.

name: Forge
founders: Not publicly disclosed founders. Hima Tammineedi is on the founding team.
what_it_does: AI automation platform that lets enterprises delegate any repetitive process to AI using human language. Creating the next layer of human-computer interaction.
problem_solved: Large companies have thousands of manual, repetitive processes that waste human time. Forge automates the most complex manual processes.
funding: Series A, backed by Founders Fund and OpenAI.
traction: On track to save customers tens of millions of dollars. Working with world's largest enterprises. High demand. Based in Manhattan Beach.
differentiator: Backed by Founders Fund AND OpenAI — rare combination. Focus on complex enterprise process automation (not just simple tasks). Forward-deployed engineering model.
delta_fellow_connection: Hima Tammineedi (Carnegie Mellon ML Masters, ex-Ramp Growth Lead, Schwarzman Scholar) is on the founding team.

name: Exa
founders: Will Bryk (CEO), Jeffrey Wang, Dan McArdle. Shreyas Sreenivas is a Founding Engineer, not a co-founder.
what_it_does: Search engine built for AIs — enables developers to filter the internet with complex queries and bring high-quality knowledge into AI applications.
problem_solved: Traditional search engines are built for humans (SEO, ads). AI applications need clean, high-quality, programmatic access to web information without noise.
funding: $85M Series B led by Benchmark at $700M valuation. $111M total raised. Nvidia (NVentures), Lightspeed, YC participated.
traction: $12M revenue (2025). 82 employees. Thousands of customers including Cursor. 144 H200 GPUs. Growing 10x valuation YoY.
differentiator: Built search from scratch using neural retrieval (not modifying Google). Novel 'Neural PageRank' approach. No ads, no SEO tricks. Fastest + highest quality for AI apps.
delta_fellow_connection: Shreyas Sreenivas (Waterloo CS, prev Volley) is a Founding Engineer.

name: Prime Intellect
founders: Johannes Hagemann, Vincent Weisser. NOTE: Ameen Patel was not found as a founder or team member.
what_it_does: Decentralized AI platform that aggregates global compute to train open-source models using distributed training across clusters on multiple continents.
problem_solved: Large-scale AI training is monopolized by Big Tech with massive GPU clusters. Prime Intellect democratizes access by distributing training across surplus global compute.
funding: $20.5M total (seed + Series A). Investors: Founders Fund, Menlo Ventures, CoinFund, Distributed Global. Angels: Andrej Karpathy, Tri Dao, Balaji Srinivasan.
traction: Trained Intellect-1 (10B params in 11 days distributed). Intellect-2 (32B with distributed RL). Intellect-3 (100B+ MoE). First distributed LLM training across 3 continents.
differentiator: Open-source decentralized training at scale. Using DiLoCo method for low-communication distributed training. Protocol for collective AI model ownership.
delta_fellow_connection: UNVERIFIED — Ameen Patel was not found connected to Prime Intellect in public sources.

name: Reve (Reve Image)
founders: Michael Garby (Michael Gharbi), Hunter Lofty. David Krajewski is a Member of Technical Staff, not a founder.
what_it_does: Advanced text-to-image generation model (Reve Image 1.0) excelling at prompt adherence, aesthetics, and typography. Also supports image-to-video via Veo 3.1 integration.
problem_solved: Existing image generation models struggle with prompt fidelity, typography, and aesthetic quality. Reve aims for industry-leading visual quality.
funding: Not publicly disclosed (Crunchbase profile exists but funding details not available).
traction: Reve Image 1.0 scored industry-leading ELO of 1159 on Artificial Analysis Image Arena. Available via Higgsfield AI, OpenArt, FLORA, fal, Replicate.
differentiator: Founded by ex-Google Brain and Nvidia experts. Top Elo score in AI image generation benchmarks. Focus on prompt adherence + typography (historically weak areas for image gen).
delta_fellow_connection: David Krajewski (Carnegie Mellon SCS, prev Niantic, Amazon) is a Member of Technical Staff.

name: Humans&
founders: Andi Peng (ex-Anthropic), Eric Zelikman (ex-xAI), Georges Harik (early Google), Yuchen He, Noah Goodman. Niloofar Mireshghallah is a Member of Technical Staff, not a founder.
what_it_does: Human-centric frontier AI lab building a new training paradigm that remembers individual preferences and emphasizes human-machine collaboration. Aims to create AI as connective tissue for organizations.
problem_solved: Current AI is designed for efficiency, not human connection. Humans& believes AI should strengthen how people collaborate and relate to each other.
funding: $480M seed at $4.48B valuation. Led by SV Angel and Georges Harik, with Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, Google Ventures, Emerson Collective. In talks for $1B at $5B valuation.
traction: Debuted as unicorn with no product yet. 20+ employees from OpenAI, Meta, Anthropic, AI2, MIT. First product planned for later in 2026.
differentiator: Massive seed ($480M) for a pre-product company. Novel training paradigm focused on memory, individual preferences, and multi-agent RL. 'Human-centric' positioning vs efficiency-first labs.
delta_fellow_connection: Niloofar Mireshghallah (ex-FAIR/Meta, UW postdoc, incoming CMU professor) is a Member of Technical Staff. Expert in AI privacy and contextual integrity.
